Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search QuickBooks for PivotPoint's invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: DocNumber "INV-2026-0315" or CustomerRef "PivotPoint") or quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: inv_pivotpoint_april_2026_dual). Find invoice totaling $10,650 with 7 line items. Line items #4-7 are Diego's specialty surcharges: custom crating $1,200, climate freight $850, interim storage $640, cargo insurance $420 = $3,110 total. Line item #7 carries a note: "Non-standard line items #4-7 require ops verification from Chloe Vance before finalizing."
OE2: Search emails with a query related to "Chloe" and "PivotPoint" or "surcharge" or "Diego" (or similar) using search_emails, and search Slack with a query related to "Chloe" and "PivotPoint" or "surcharge verification" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages. Find no evidence in any service that Chloe Vance has responded or verified the surcharge line items. The flag Catalina sent to Chloe remains unanswered.
OE3: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true or criteria for "Heartland" or "Swift"). Find 19 bills total. Discover that no PivotPoint-specific vendor bills exist yet for the current dual relocation. The SWR-2026-04-PATEL bill ($4,980) is for NorthWind's Raj Patel, not PivotPoint. Agent must note the absence and estimate vendor costs from rate schedules rather than actual bills.
OE4: Search emails with a query related to "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" or "rate increase" or "DOT surcharge" (or similar) using search_emails. Find Greg Pallone's email (April 11, 2026) announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new bookings plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, effective immediately.
OE5: Search emails with a query related to "Jake Loomis" or "Heartland" or "rate increase" or "Q3 rate" (or similar) using search_emails. Find Jake Loomis's formal notice (April 13, 2026) announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective Q3, tied to the unresolved invoice dispute HM-2026-Q1-0042. Heartland will not finalize rates until the dispute is resolved.
OE6: Search Linear with a query related to "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1" or "vendor reconciliation" (or similar) using linear_list_issues, or retrieve the issue directly using linear_get_issue (id: issue_heartland_q1_recon). Find ticket OPS-342 "Vendor invoice reconciliation - Heartland Movers Q1 2026" still in state In Progress. $4,800 overbilling on HM-2026-Q1-0042 documented, formal dispute sent by Marcus, no corrected invoice received from Heartland. Optionally search for a related Linear issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "billing discrepancies") to find Hana's expanded review noting additional discrepancies on Kevin Tran and Priya Nair beyond the original three disputed items.
OE7: Search Slack with a query related to "vendor exposure" or "account-specific risk" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages, or retrieve the channel history of C002 (#customer-engagement) using conversations_history (channel_id: C002). Find David Chen's message (ts: 1776370500.000000) asking AMs to map client exposure to vendor rate changes.
OE8: Retrieve thread replies from David's vendor exposure thread using conversations_replies (channel_id: C002, thread_ts: 1776370500.000000). Find 3 replies: (a) Mina Hashimoto covering Vectral Systems (trust-fragile) and BrightLoop Analytics (volume-heavy, DOT surcharge already hitting); (b) Emeka Diallo covering Mosaic Robotics (specialized handling, yellow trending red); (c) David Chen's follow-up (ts: 1776393840.000000) specifically requesting "Midwest account coverage from Catalina," naming NorthWind, Prairie Data, and Iron Harbor.
OE9: Search Airtable for Catalina's active relocations using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) or airtable_search_records (field_name: Status, value: "In Progress"). Find active moves across Catalina's accounts. Cross-reference with CRM deals and invoice data to identify vendor assignments: NorthWind (Raj Patel on Swift for hazmat server transport, Emilia Cruz on Heartland for household goods), PivotPoint (Diego Castaneda on Swift for CNC equipment crating and climate freight, Nadia Okonjo's household goods vendor not yet confirmed), Canopy (Dr. Kovacs on Swift, Marcus Webb split-vendor Heartland plus Swift for cold-chain, Tyler Okonkwo vendor TBD), Prairie Data (Tyler Briggs completed).
OE10: Search QuickBooks for the Canopy invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: DocNumber "INV-2026-0401" or CustomerRef "Canopy") or search emails with a query related to "Canopy surcharge" or "Patricia Langford" (or similar) using search_emails. Find Canopy invoice INV-2026-0401 totaling $9,210 with three surcharge line items pending client approval from Patricia Langford: $850 TN visa/customs broker (Kovacs), $1,200 cold-chain freight (Webb), $600 temp storage (Okonkwo) = $2,650 total pending.
OE11: Search CRM for PivotPoint deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: company_pivot) or crm_get_deal (id: deal_pivot_april_2026_dual). Find the existing deal "PivotPoint Labs - April 2026 Dual Relocation" at stage "inprogress" with amount $10,650. Verify whether this stage still accurately reflects the current situation.
OE12: Post a thread reply in David's #customer-engagement thread using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002, thread_ts: 1776370500.000000). Content must include: (a) account-specific vendor exposure for Catalina's accounts (NorthWind, PivotPoint, Canopy, Prairie Data); (b) which moves use Heartland vs Swift; (c) acknowledgment of Mina's and Emeka's prior coverage so as not to repeat their work; (d) Canopy surcharge approval status; (e) Heartland dispute resolution status.
OE13: Create a new CRM pipeline deal for PivotPoint summer relocations using crm_create_deal (dealname: "PivotPoint summer relocations" or similar, company_id: company_pivot, pipeline: relocation-services, dealstage: qualifiedtobuy or appointmentscheduled).
OE14: Update PivotPoint CRM deal notes with invoice and vendor findings using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE, body covering invoice status, vendor risks, surcharge sign-off gap, company_ids: ["company_pivot"]).
OE15: Look up Sam Delgado's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Sam" or "Delgado" or "PivotPoint"). Find sam@pivotpointlabs.com, Co-founder & CEO, (512) 555-0190.
OE16: Send an email from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to sam@pivotpointlabs.com using send_email. Content must include: (a) check-in on Nadia Okonjo's move status (H-1B coordination, SF to Austin); (b) check-in on Diego Castaneda's move status (CNC equipment, Brooklyn to Austin); (c) invoice INV-2026-0315 timeline confirmation relative to PivotPoint's April 30 fiscal year close; (d) summer relocations follow-up to keep the conversation going.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
